chapter_context.summary: Records Baruch writing Jeremiah's prophecies on a roll, Jehoiakim mockingly cutting and burning it, and God commanding Jeremiah to write an expanded roll.

outline.text: Baruch Writing Jeremiah's Prophecies and Reading inside Temple
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Baruch Writing Jeremiah's Prophecies and Reading inside Temple (36:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Roll Read to Princes, Who Advice Baruch and Jeremiah to Hide
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Roll Read to Princes, Who Advice Baruch and Jeremiah to Hide (36:9-19), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: King Jehoiakim Burning Prophetic Roll Page by Page
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Jehoiakim Burning Prophetic Roll Page by Page (36:20-26), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

outline.text: Writing Second Roll with Expanded Prophecies of Judgment
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Writing Second Roll with Expanded Prophecies of Judgment (36:27-32), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Jeremiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Conflict and Exile
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of conflict and exile, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Weeping Prophet
key_themes.description: Jeremiah's deep emotional connection to the message and the people, illustrating the heart of God breaking for His children's rebellion.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and historical accounts of Chapter 36, in what ways does Jeremiah's call to faithfulness encourage you in your own walk today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose covenant obedience and trust in God's promises of restoration over temporary security?

cultural_historical.title: The Fall of Jerusalem and the Exile
cultural_historical.text: The historical backdrop of Jeremiah's ministry, witnessing the gradual collapse of Judah under the Babylonian Empire and the destruction of the Temple.
